pattern: Three points form a boomerang exactly when the signed area of their triangle is non-zero, which a single cross-product test decides without division.
hints: Three collinear points have zero area; you only need to detect when the area vanishes.
The cross product of the two vectors leaving the first point tells you whether they point the same way.
Compute (x2-x1)*(y3-y1) - (y2-y1)*(x3-x1); the points are a boomerang precisely when this is not zero.

stress.description: random small triangles, often collinear or coincident over a tiny grid
stress.note: tiny grid makes collinear and coincident cases frequent
